narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your undergrad work and LSAT prep—it's tough to hear that your application to law school didn't make the cut because our model sees a 43% chance you might struggle with the bar exam down the line. Picture it like this: the model looked at your story as a whole, and the biggest chapters that stood out were your Year 3 grades, placing you in the 8th decile at school—that was the strongest signal pulling toward potential challenges, suggesting the demands of upper-level courses might have been a steeper climb for you. Right behind that was your Year 1 performance in the 7th decile, reinforcing that early hurdles in law school rigor could carry over. Your low family income added another layer, as it often means fewer resources to lean on during those grueling study marathons. Then there's your LSAT score of 19, which didn't quite shine as brightly as the model hoped for predicting bar success, and your Asian background factored in as tilting the odds a bit toward difficulty in this context. Your solid 3.2 undergrad GPA pulled some weight too, but not enough to counterbalance the rest. Being full-time is the path most take, yet it nudged things toward risk in your profile. The one bright spot was your gender—female applicants like you often show real grit that sways things toward passing in patterns we've seen before. It's this blend—stronger early and late deciles signaling academic fit issues, layered with income and test score realities—that painted a picture of higher risk for the bar, even if you're determined and capable in ways numbers can't fully capture. This isn't the end of your story; many with similar profiles have pivoted, boosted their prep, and succeeded elsewhere—maybe retaking the LSAT or targeting schools with stronger support for students from low-income backgrounds could rewrite the next chapter for you. You've got the drive; now it's about finding the right spotlight. Hang in there.
